sourceMovie.title: Melancholia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Melancholia for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Lars von Trier keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mr. Nobody
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Melancholia clicked for you, Mr. Nobody (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Melancholia?
faq.answer: Try Mr. Nobody, Another Earth, Enemy Mine—each shares DNA with Melancholia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Melancholia?
faq.answer: Melancholia (2011) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Melancholia worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
